sourceTitle: Migrate from Confluence to Notion and transform your wiki into a connected workspace

questions.question: Which of the following is a primary advantage of using Notion for a team wiki compared to Confluence, as described in the guide?

options.text: Notion automatically translates all content into multiple languages.
options.explanation: The guide does not mention automatic translation as a benefit.

options.text: Notion lets you connect documentation directly with daily work items like projects and roadmaps.
options.explanation: The guide highlights that Notion puts docs and work in the same place, enabling seamless navigation between them.

options.text: Notion requires no learning curve for technical teams.
options.explanation: While Notion is user‑friendly, the guide does not claim there is zero learning curve.

options.text: Notion provides built‑in video conferencing for wiki collaboration.
options.explanation: Video conferencing is not listed as a feature in the migration guide.

questions.question: When should you choose the API importer over the file (zip) importer?

options.text: When you only need to import a handful of pages and no comments.
options.explanation: The API importer is meant for full‑space migrations, not just a few pages.

options.text: When the Confluence space is under 15 GB, you want to migrate users and comments, and you’re moving the entire space.
options.explanation: The guide lists these exact conditions for using the API importer.

options.text: When the Confluence space exceeds 20 GB.
options.explanation: The guide caps the API importer at 15 GB; larger spaces would need a different approach.

options.text: When you prefer to manually edit the HTML files before importing.
options.explanation: Manual editing is not a reason to pick the API importer.

questions.question: What happens if a Confluence user does not already have a Notion account during an API import?

options.text: The import fails and stops immediately.
options.explanation: The guide says the import continues; placeholders are created for missing users.

options.text: A placeholder account is created until the user signs up for Notion.
options.explanation: Placeholders ensure comments and ownership are preserved until the user joins Notion.

options.text: The user’s comments are omitted from the import.
options.explanation: Comments are kept; missing users are represented by placeholders.

options.text: Notion sends an automatic invitation email to the missing user.
options.explanation: While Notion may send an email after import, the guide specifically mentions placeholders, not automatic invites.

questions.question: During a zip‑file import, which file format must you export from Confluence?

options.explanation: PDF is not the required format for the zip import.
The guide instructs users to select HTML before exporting a zip file.
Markdown is not mentioned as an export option for this process.
DOCX is not part of the described workflow.

questions.question: Which Notion feature allows you to signal that a wiki page’s information is current until a specific date?

options.explanation: Versioning tracks changes but does not display a validity date.
The guide describes page verification as a way to mark content as up‑to‑date until a set date.
Locking prevents edits but does not convey a date‑based status.
Reminders notify users but do not certify the page’s accuracy.

questions.question: If you want to embed a diagram created in Draw.io into a Notion page, what is the recommended approach according to the guide?

options.text: Use Notion’s native Draw.io integration block.
options.explanation: The guide states Notion does not yet have a direct integration with Draw.io.

options.text: Export the diagram as an image and embed the image file.
options.explanation: Since direct integration isn’t available, the guide suggests embedding the diagram as an image.

options.text: Copy the SVG code and paste it into a code block.
options.explanation: Embedding SVG via a code block isn’t mentioned as a supported method.

options.text: Upload the Draw.io file as a PDF and link to it.
options.explanation: While possible, the guide specifically recommends using an image format.

questions.question: Which of the following Notion blocks can best replace Confluence’s “Expand” feature?

options.explanation: Callouts are used for highlighted messages, not for collapsible content.
The guide explicitly states toggles replace Confluence’s Expand feature.
Quote blocks display quoted text and do not provide expand/collapse functionality.
Dividers are visual separators, not interactive expanders.

questions.question: What is the maximum size allowed for a Confluence space when using the API importer?

options.explanation: 5 GB is the limit for the file (zip) importer, not the API importer.
The guide does not list 10 GB as a threshold.
The API importer can be used for spaces smaller than 15 GB, per the guide.

options.text: Unlimited – there is no size restriction.
options.explanation: A size limit of 15 GB is explicitly mentioned.

questions.question: Which Notion feature enables you to keep content synchronized across multiple pages, ensuring changes in one place update everywhere?

options.explanation: Linked databases create new views, not automatic content sync across pages.
The guide describes copying content into a synced block so updates propagate.
Template buttons generate new content but do not keep existing blocks in sync.
Relations link records between databases, not synchronize page content.

questions.question: If you need to quickly draft a new wiki page or improve existing copy, which Notion tool does the guide recommend?

options.explanation: The guide suggests using Notion AI via the spacebar to generate or edit wiki content.
While possible, the guide does not promote an external editor for this purpose.
Copy‑paste works but isn’t highlighted as the recommended quick‑draft tool.
Voice dictation isn’t mentioned in the guide.
